data.title: Despite Tariff Turmoil, Bank Of America CEO Says Consumer Is 'Continuing To Spend' (UPDATED)
data.description: Bank of America reported strong Q1 earnings, with net income of $7.4B and EPS of $0.90, beating analyst estimates. Revenue and net interest income also increased. Stock has declined 17% this year, but may see relief due to US-China tariff pause.

data.title: Why Is Grocery Giant Albertsons' Stock Price Shrinking Today?
data.description: Albertsons (NYSE: ACI) posted strong Q4 sales but weak margins. Revenue reached $18.80 billion, topping estimates, driven by a 2.3% rise in identical sales and 24% digital growth. Despite the sales beat, operating income plunged 35%. For FY25, the grocer expects EPS to be below the analyst forecasts.

data.title: Netflix, Ericsson, Hewlett Packard Enterprise And Other Big Stocks Moving Higher On Monday
US Manufacturing Unlikely To Benefit From Tariffs, Survey Indicates: Majority Feels Trump Team Is 'Bullying Corporate America'
data.description: A survey has indicated that the tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump's administration may not lead to a resurgence in U.S. manufacturing.

data.title: Bessent Says Tariffs On China Are 'Far From A Joke,' Dismisses Treasury Dumping Fears
data.description: Treasury Sec. Bessent confident in trade talks & US markets despite volatility. Fast-moving agenda to reshape trade & keep hard line on China.
